title: Soya Chunk Masala

subs.name: tofu (diced firm tofu, dry-fried before adding to masala) — same protein, slightly milder flavour
2 tbsp tomato paste mixed in 50 ml water — richer colour and saves time
ghee — adds richness but is not vegan

steps.text: Bring a pot of water to the boil, add a pinch of salt and the soya chunks. Cook for 5 minutes until they puff up and soften. Drain, then squeeze out all the absorbed water by pressing each chunk between your palms — this step is essential, otherwise the chunks water down the masala and stay spongy rather than absorbing the curry.

Heat oil in a kadai on medium-high. Add the bay leaf and let it sizzle for 10 seconds. Add onion and sauté, stirring often, for 8-10 minutes until deep golden — this long sauté is the flavour backbone of the dish.

Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilli; fry for 1-2 minutes until the raw smell goes. Add the pureed tomatoes with a pinch of salt and cook on medium for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until the masala is thick and the oil surfaces around the edges.
Lower the flame. Add turmeric, red chilli powder and coriander powder, stir for 30 seconds. Tip in the squeezed soya chunks and toss to coat completely in the masala.

Pour in 150 ml water, bring to a gentle simmer, cover and cook on medium-low for 8-10 minutes until the chunks have absorbed the gravy and the oil separates on top. Stir in garam masala, check salt, garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot with chapati or steamed rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: soya-chunks → tofu (diced firm tofu, dry-fried before adding to masala) — same protein, slightly milder flavour; tomato → 2 tbsp tomato paste mixed in 50 ml water — richer colour and saves time; oil → ghee — adds richness but is not vegan
